content: read reviews/55.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/55.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/55.md.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/55.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (line 19).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 55`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source: `[erdosproblems.com/55](https://www.erdosproblems.com/55)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (Ramsey complete sets with counting bound).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[CFP21]` citing Conlon, Fox, and Pham (2021).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos55 ... end Erdos55`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos55`.
- [x] `open Finset Real` appears **before** the namespace block (line 32, before line 34).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `IsRamseyComplete` has a LaTeX docstring (lines 36ÔÇô40).
- [x] No `noncomputable` needed ÔÇö definition is a `Prop`-valued function, no classical choice involved.
- [x] No `sorry` in the definition.
- [x] Type name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `IsRamseyComplete`.
- N/A: No local notation used; none needed.
- N/A: No `@[category test]` / `@[category API]` sanity checks added; optional but could be considered.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Both declarations use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_55` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Names follow Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 48ÔÇô52) referencing `[CFP21]`.
- [x] Variant has a docstring (lines 64ÔÇô68) referencing `[CFP21]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem is stated as a direct assertion: "For every r Ôëą 2, there exists an r-Ramsey complete set A with |A Ôłę {1,ÔÇŽ,N}| ÔëĄ C┬Ěr┬Ě(log N)┬▓, and this bound is best possible." This is **Case 3** (direct assertion), so no `answer()` wrapper is needed.

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly ÔÇö no `answer()` wrapper. Correct for Case 3.
- N/A: Problem is solved affirmatively, so no negation needed.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
- [x] Value is valid: `research solved` (problem solved by Conlon, Fox, and Pham but not formally verified in Lean).
- [x] Category is appropriate: `research solved` with `sorry` in proof body is correct (the result is known but not yet formally proved).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5 11`.
- [x] Numbers are in **ascending numeric order**: 5, 11.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both are valid AMS MSC2020 codes: `5` (Combinatorics) and `11` (Number theory).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Both theorems use `by` + `sorry` (multi-line form: `:= by\n  sorry`), which is acceptable.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- N/A: Not an open problem, so no concern about informal proof sketches.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variant named `erdos_55.variants.lower_bound` ÔÇö follows the `erdos_N.variants.<descriptor>` convention.
- [x] Variant lives in the **same file** as the main theorem.
- [x] Variant has its own `@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]` attributes.
- [x] Variant has a docstring explaining it shows the upper bound is best possible.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ÔłÇ`, `Ôłâ`, `Ôłł`, `ÔŐć`, `ÔäĽ`, `ÔäŁ`, `Ôëą`, `ÔëĄ`, `┬Č`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run as part of this review ÔÇö requires manual verification.
- [ ] Custom linter passes not verified ÔÇö requires build.

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (pending build verification)

The file is clean and fully conformant with the checklist. Both the main theorem and the lower-bound variant are well-structured, properly attributed, correctly categorized as `research solved`, and include docstrings with paper references. The custom definition `IsRamseyComplete` is well-documented and correctly typed. No issues found.

content.text: The review passed ("**Result: PASS**"). No changes needed.
